title: quercus × jackiana
intro: Quercus × jackiana, also known as the hybrid of the white oak (Quercus alba) and the California black oak (Quercus kelloggii), is a deciduous tree native to the western coast of North America. The bark and leaves of Quercus × jackiana have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus × jackiana include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Quercus × jackiana typically involve infusions or decoctions of the bark and leaves, which can be consumed as teas or used as topical applications.
